ns1:dc.dc:title: Novel Injector Technology with Advanced Modular Conceptual Design to Achieve Superior Material Feed in Jet Milling Systems
ns1:dc.dc:creator: Kopp, Johannes
ns1:dc.dc:subject: ddc:60
ns1:dc.dc:description: This paper explores a new principle for counter jet milling, whereby material is fed precisely guided into the free jet of Laval nozzles. By developing this concept, elaborating a modular prototype, and using rapid prototyping, calcium carbonate's feeding and milling behaviour in a multiphase flow was analysed under real conditions. Through experimental studies, the advantages of the next-generation system compared to classical feeding utilising a dosing screw were proven to be considerably more efficient. In addition, it was possible to determine actual firing zones using delamination tests performed on the MultiNo¨ 1050 counter jet mill manufactured by Aufbereitungstechnologie Noll GmbH, Bobingen, which represents a key basis for future optimisations and studies relating to large-scale industrial grinding processes.

ns1:dc.dc:title: Effect of Drilling Parameters on the Machining Quality of Ceramic Matrix Composites
ns1:dc.dc:creator: Kraus, Manuel
ns1:dc.dc:subject: ddc:62
ns1:dc.dc:description: Ceramic matrix composites (CMCs) are innovative materials that are increasingly being used in demanding applications such as aerospace and energy technology due to their high temperature resistance, strength and low density. Due to their high hardness, brittleness and abrasive behavior, these materials represent particular challenges in terms of processing, which is why specific strategies and adapted process parameters are required. An important topic in research is the energy saving in manufacturing processes of CMCs. In order to achieve this goal, a correlation between process parameters and component quality is developed in this paper.

ns1:dc.dc:title: Technological Concept Development and Determination of Repair Strategies and Rules for C/SiC-Materials
ns1:dc.dc:creator: Kuntzsch, Maximilian
ns1:dc.dc:subject: ddc:62
ns1:dc.dc:description: The manufacturing process of ceramic matrix composites is an energy consuming and expensive procedure. Resources could be saved by repairing damaged components. Due to the high difficulty and the unpredictable extent of damage, the repair is usually not possible. In this work, repair concepts and rules are established and systematically tested on a C/SiC demonstrator. The results show that the repair method depends on the position and geometry of the damage. For example, an inlay should be created for a damaged rib, whereas an infill should be done for a surface damage. In extreme cases, the damage may extend across the entire component, resulting in complete structural failure. Repairing such damage is significantly more complex, as the reconstruction may require the assembly of numerous fragments while maintaining the original geometric and dimensional accuracy.

ns1:dc.dc:title: Website Taxonomy Extraction – Identifying and Extracting Navigation Structures from Websites
ns1:dc.dc:creator: Voigt, Ralph
ns1:dc.dc:subject: ddc:004
ns1:dc.dc:description: Publishers of websites want their information to be as accessible as possible to visitors. How­ever, the navigation menus on websites nowadays have become quite complex and limit this accessibility of information. In order to pave a way for browser extensions that are able to transform the rigid navigation in terms of both the layout and the structure, the taxonomy embedded in the HTML-navigation must be extracted first. This thesis will show that it is possible to identify the root node of a multi layer website navigation in O( [links in the DOM tree] * [height of the DOM tree] ) time and extract the embedded taxonomy in O( [links in the navigation tree] * [height of the navigation tree] ) time, while analyzing only the startpage of a website. In order to obtain a complete overview of the website, the algorithm goes on by merging all links of subsequent webpages into the context of the taxonomy extracted from the startpage. By keeping the hierarchical information intact it enables a completely new approach to develop­ing applications for improving accessibility and usability of website navigation: The navigation structure can now be reproduced and enhanced in terms of markup, layout and functionality. lt also opens context based semantics of navigation taxonomies as a new research field, which will yield new solutions for better understandable information architectures.

ns1:dc.dc:title: Characterization of Short-Fiber-Reinforced C/SiC with Recycled C-Fibers
ns1:dc.dc:creator: Mayr, Ramona Daniela
ns1:dc.dc:subject: ddc:62
ns1:dc.dc:description: This study evaluates the suitability of recycled carbon fibers (rCF) as reinforcement material in short fiber-reinforced C/C-SiC ceramics. The composite was fabricated using industrially recycled carbon fibers and a phenolic resin as matrix precursor at a fiber volume content of 50 %. Samples were processed via liquid silicon infiltration (LSI). Characterization included microstructural analysis using ceramic cross-sections and digital microscopy, as well as mechanical testing through three-point bending tests according to DIN EN 658-3. The commercial reference material FU 2952/9 P77 from Schunk Carbon Technology was used for comparison. Results indicate that the rCF-based composite achieves a comparable average flexural strength of 49.5 MPa to the industrial reference material. Typical fiber-reinforced ceramic features such as damage tolerance and fiber pull-out were confirmed. The study highlights the potential of recycled carbon fibers for sustainable high-performance composite materials.

ns1:dc.dc:title: The Sustainability Monitor of Augsburg Region - Second Survey Round
ns1:dc.dc:creator: Notzon, Natalie
ns1:dc.dc:subject: ddc:33
ns1:dc.dc:description: The Augsburg Sustainability Monitor is an instrument for recording and evaluating the sustainability activities of companies, institutions and organizations in the A³ region, consisting of the City of Augsburg, Augsburg County and Aichach-Friedberg County. The project is carried out by Regio Augsburg Wirtschaft GmbH in cooperation with Technical University of Applied Sciences Augsburg and is funded by the Bavarian State Ministry of Finance and Home Affairs. The aim of the project is to assess the current status of sustainable development, to identify challenges and opportunities and to derive concrete recommendations for action and support services for the region. At the same time, it will strengthen the A³ region as a sustainable business location and make future development measurable. The online survey with 57 questions, which is based on international standards such as the DNK, GRI, SDGs, ESRS and ISO 26000, is conducted on an annual basis. The results are used to develop specific recommendations for action for the individual organizations, a comparison over time and comparisons between other characteristics such as the sector. The recommendations for action relate to the promotion of sustainability in the A3 region.

ns1:dc.dc:title: Natural Lighting in Rooms - Method to Collect Training Data for AI Application
ns1:dc.dc:creator: Eberl, Florian
ns1:dc.dc:subject: ddc:72

ns1:dc.dc:title: A Digital Methodology for the Automation of Environmental Product Declarations and Lifecycle Data Management
ns1:dc.dc:creator: Ecker, Philipp
ns1:dc.dc:subject: ddc:72
ns1:dc.dc:description: The creation of Environmental Product Declarations (EPDs) poses a significant hurdle for small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) due to high costs and complexity. This paper introduces a conceptual framework for a web-based, AI-assisted system designed to overcome these barriers. The central contribution is the design of a pragmatic, document-based approach that does not require complex Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) integration. It utilizes a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) architecture to power two specialized AI assistants: one for extracting data from unstructured documents and another for generating narrative EPD reports. This transforms the EPD from a static output into a dynamic, machine-readable asset. The proposed framework aims to make the EPD process more accessible, efficient, and transparent, enabling manufacturers to actively use Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) data for product optimization.

ns1:dc.dc:title: The Sustainable Revitalization of the Former Residential and Industrial Tower on Baumgartnerstraße: A Process Description
ns1:dc.dc:creator: Nieberle, Marina
ns1:dc.dc:subject: ddc:72
ns1:dc.dc:description: This paper documents the ongoing sustainable revitalization process of a historic residential and industrial tower on Baumgartnerstraße in Augsburg through the "Green-Heritage360°" project. The 1926-built structure, vacant since 2009, serves as a real-world laboratory demonstrating how participatory planning methodologies can transform underutilized heritage into active community assets through interim use concepts, successive restoration strategies, and stakeholder co-creation processes. Operating as a Living Lab, the initiative integrates ecological, social, and economic sustainability dimensions while fostering collaboration between heritage conservation specialists, academic institutions, and municipal partners. The comprehensive stakeholder network analysis reveals how diverse actors contribute essential expertise to the collaborative revitalization effort, challenging traditional top-down urban planning through bottom-up approaches that emphasize equality and local participation. Initial activation phases include establishing a student-run café using recycled materials and reversible construction methods, alongside academic integration through interdisciplinary coursework. The project aims to develop transferable models for climate-friendly revitalization of vacant heritage buildings while advancing novel educational concepts through digital knowledge exchange platforms.

ns1:dc.dc:title: Lean Management in Practice: A Case Study on Process Optimization and Digitization in Yard Management
ns1:dc.dc:creator: Hössl, Timo
ns1:dc.dc:subject: ddc:33
ns1:dc.dc:description: This paper presents a case study on the application of lean management in yard logistics at a regional construction company. Using the design science research method, key inefficiencies were identified through process mapping and performance analysis. Lean tools such as 5S, Kanban, and Visual Management were proposed to optimize material handling and improve transparency. While the focus lies on supporting logistics processes, the approach is closely related to lean construction management and can optimally— and in some cases necessarily—support lean execution on construction projects. The study demonstrates the relevance of lean management for small and medium-sized enterprises in construction logistics and highlights the importance of stakeholder involvement and process visualization.

ns1:dc.dc:title: Systemic Influence of Macroeconomic Factors on German Used Textile Collectors and Sorters
ns1:dc.dc:creator: Hatfield, Sarah (Prof. Dr.)
Jahn, Svenja
Hühn, Nicole
ns1:dc.dc:subject: ddc:33
ns1:dc.dc:description: The German textile collection and sorting system is facing mounting ecological, economic, and regulatory pressures. This paper utilizes a PESTEL analysis and a Vester Sensitivity Model to examine the sector's primary challenges. The analysis, which is based on 32 influencing factors, identifies textile recycling and infrastructure as critical levers for sustainable transformation. The findings provide a systemic perspective on interdependencies and support strategic decision-making in the context of the NuCollect research project.

ns1:dc.dc:title: Experimental Frequency Analysis of an Axial Flux Motor Component
ns1:dc.dc:creator: Bersiner, Paul
Baeten, André (Prof. Dr.-Ing.)
ns1:dc.dc:subject: ddc:62
ns1:dc.dc:description: In this paper, the vibration behavior of a component of an axial flux motor is to be measured. The results obtained from this are to serve as simulation validation for subsequent work to be able to simulate the dynamic behavior of the component with regards to fatigue strength. To evaluate the data, a MATLAB script is developed that uses the Fourier transform to visualize the natural frequencies of the component by means of a representation in the frequency domain and can calculate the transfer function of the measured component by dividing the determined power spectra. A numerical simulation of a two-mass oscillator is used to prove the qualitative accuracy of the MATLAB script. The data from a single series of measurements are analyzed and it is shown that the strong damping of the measurement setup has a strong influence on the data and therefore no causal relationship between the input and output signal can be proven.

ns1:dc.dc:title: Implementation of a Radar System into an Automated Vehicle
ns1:dc.dc:creator: Thuringer, Anna
Markgraf, Carsten (Prof. Dr.-Ing.)
ns1:dc.dc:subject: ddc:62
ns1:dc.dc:description: This paper presents the mechanical and electrical integration of a 4D imaging radar sensor into a vehicle platform, as part of a broader effort to enable advanced driver-assistance systems. A suitable sensor placement strategy was developed using simulation-based analysis, considering mounting height and orientation to optimize environmental coverage and minimize blind spots. A custom sensor mount was designed and manufactured using ASA material, to ensure thermal and mechanical robustness. The radome was designed based on electromagnetic transparency criteria, with PE selected for its favorable dielectric properties. Electrical integration involved adapting Automotive Ethernet via a media converter to interface with an onboard PC. The described integration provides a foundation for future real-world testing and further expansions of the sensor network.

ns1:dc.dc:title: KI im Arbeitsalltag: Zusammenhänge zwischen der Wahrnehmung künstlicher Intelligenz und dem Wohlbefinden von Mitarbeitenden
ns1:dc.dc:creator: Weber, Jana
ns1:dc.dc:subject: ddc:33
ns1:dc.dc:description: Die zunehmende Integration von KI im Arbeitsalltag wirft wichtige Fragen bezüglich ihrer Auswirkungen auf das Wohlbefinden von Mitarbeitenden auf. Diese Studie untersucht den Zusammenhang zwischen der Wahrnehmung von KI-Systemen am Arbeitsplatz und dem psychologischen Wohlbefinden. Dabei wird die vermittelnde Rolle der psychologischen Grundbedürfnisse nach Autonomie, Kompetenz und Verbundenheit genauer betrachtet. Zur Überprüfung der Forschungsfrage wurde eine quantitative Online-Erhebung durchgeführt. 82 erwerbstätige Personen wurden befragt, die regelmäßig KI bei der Arbeit nutzen. Die Ergebnisse bestätigen einen positiven Zusammenhang zwischen KI-Wahrnehmung und PERMA-Wohlbefinden. Sowohl die Nutzungsintensität als auch Nutzungsdauer führen zu einer positiveren KI-Wahrnehmung. Diese wiederum fördert alle drei psychologischen Grundbedürfnisse, wobei das Autonomieerleben am stärksten beeinflusst wird. Entgegen der Erwartungen mediieren die Grundbedürfnisse jedoch nicht den Effekt zwischen KIWahrnehmung und Wohlbefinden. Die Studie zeigt, dass subjektive Bewertungsprozesse von KI-Systemen für das Mitarbeitendenwohlbefinden von zentraler Bedeutung sind. Allerdings gelten für diesen Zusammenhang andere Wirkmechanismen, die über motivationstheoretische Ansätze hinaus gehen.

The increasing integration of AI in the workplace raises important questions regarding its effects on employee well-being. This study examines the relationship between the perception of AI systems at work and psychological well-being, with particular focus on the mediating role of the basic psychological needs for autonomy, competence, and relatedness. To address the research question, a quantitative online survey was conducted with 82 employed individuals who regularly use AI at work. The results confirm a positive relationship between AI perception and PERMA well-being. Both usage intensity and duration lead to more positive AI perception, which in turn promotes all three basic psychological needs. Contrary to expectations, the basic needs do not mediate the effect between AI perception and wellbeing. The study demonstrates that subjective evaluation processes of AI systems are of central importance for employee well-being. However, different mechanisms apply to this relationship that extend beyond motivational theoretical approaches.

ns1:dc.dc:title: Eine theoretische Reflexion über Straßenzeitungen als soziales Phänomen im Neoliberalismus
ns1:dc.dc:creator: Rottländer, Samuel
ns1:dc.dc:subject: ddc:360
ns1:dc.dc:description: Die vorliegende Bachelorarbeit untersucht im Rahmen einer theoretischen Reflexion Straßenzeitungen als ein soziales Phänomen im Neoliberalismus. Ausgangspunkt ist die Frage, inwiefern Straßenzeitungen den Umgang mit Stigmata und Empowerment von armutsbetroffenen Menschen im Kontext neoliberaler Strukturen gestalten können. Armut und soziale Ungleichheit werden dabei als grundlegende Bestandteile des kapitalistisch funktionierenden Sozialstaats angesehen. Zunächst werden Straßenzeitungen und deren Bedeutung für die Verkäufer*innen theoretisch eingeordnet. Darauf aufbauend wird die Ambivalenz von Empowerment und Stigmatisierung im Straßenzeitungsverkauf näher betrachtet. Abschließend wird der Rolle von Straßenzeitungen in neoliberalen Strukturen aus Perspektive der marxistischen Gesellschaftstheorie nachgegangen. Vor diesem Hintergrund wird deutlich, dass Straßenzeitungsprojekte auf individueller Ebene einen großen Einfluss auf die Lebensrealitäten der Adressat*innen haben können. Auf einer systemischen Ebene stehen die Projekte und ihre Verkäufer*innen zunehmend von vielen Seiten unter Druck, was sich stark auf die Möglichkeiten des Empowerments und der Partizipation im Rahmen entsprechender Straßenzeitungsprojekten auswirken kann.

ns1:dc.dc:title: Effects of a Voice Assistant's Gender-Identity on the Fulfillment of Basic Psychological Needs
ns1:dc.dc:creator: Polly, Kathrin Juliana
ns1:dc.dc:subject: ddc:330
ns1:dc.dc:description: Building on the frameworks of the Basic Psychological Needs Theory and the Technology Acceptance Model, this study examines how the perceived gender of a digital voice assistant (VA) influences users‘ perception of basic psychological need (BPN) fulfillment and their intention to use (ITU) a voice-based system. In a between- subjects experimental design, participants (n=83) were presented a fictional finance voice assistant displaying either a clearly feminine, or a gender-ambiguous voice. Results showed that overall BPN fulfillment and competence satisfaction were significantly higher among participants in the female-voiced condition, while ITU, autonomy satisfaction, and relatedness satisfaction did not differ significantly between the conditions. Additionally, BPN fulfillment was strongly positively correlated with ITU. Furthermore, male participants reported significantly higher Affinity for Technology Interaction (ATI) than female participants, though ATI did not significantly correlate with any dependent variables. These findings highlight both, the potential and the limitations of gender-ambiguous voice design as a strategy to reduce gender bias in human-VA interaction, without significantly comprimising user engagement. This study further emphasizes the importance of considering basic psychological need satisfaction in the context of shaping and evaluating user experience.

ns1:dc.dc:title: Assessing RLVR’s Efficacy in Solving Previously Intractable Problems with LLMs
ns1:dc.dc:creator: Uhlmann, Julius
ns1:dc.dc:subject: ddc:004
ns1:dc.dc:description: Reinforcement Learning from Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) has emerged as a promising method for enhancing the reasoning capabilities of large language models (LLMs). There is an ongoing debate, however, as to whether RLVR genuinely expands reasoning capacities in LLMs and enables the emergence of new strategies in a manner similar to that of traditional RL agents. Seeking to objectively evaluate behavioral emergence in RLVR, this thesis assesses RLVR’s efficacy in solving previously intractable problems. By analyzing various relevant and contemporary implementations, this work shows that problems that cannot be solved by a base model often become solvable through RLVR training. Even though many of these problems are highly constructed, this shift represents evidence of a genuine expansion of reasoning capabilities through reinforcement learning. A deeper understanding of the limitations of current methods will enable further advances in research and the development of LLMs with greater success in complex reasoning tasks.

ns1:dc.dc:title: Taxonomie für Datenmanagement-Ansätze
ns1:dc.dc:creator: Brindöpke, Marie
Prinz, Niculin
ns1:dc.dc:subject: ddc:004
ns1:dc.dc:description: Die Digitalisierung stellt Unternehmen vor die Herausforderungen ihre Daten strategisch zu managen und sie dafür in ihre Unternehmensarchitektur zu integrieren. Obwohl Datenmanagement als Disziplin nicht neu ist, ist der Reifegrad in der Praxis immer noch niedrig. Daher untersucht dieser Beitrag, wie Unternehmen Datenmanagement aktuell umsetzen und entwickelt auf Basis von fünf Expert*inneninterviews eine Taxonomie für Datenmanagement-Ansätze. Diese Taxonomie zeigt die Bandbereite der praktischen Ansätze durch neun abgeleitete Charakteristika mit verschiedenen Ausprägungen auf. Sie trägt für ein besseres Verständnis in Wissenschaft und Praxis bei und kann als Basis für eine tiefergehende Analyse von Datenmanagement dienen.

ns1:dc.dc:title: Adoption of the Enterprise Architecture Function within the Transformation of the IT Function of a Global Enterprise
ns1:dc.dc:creator: Buchner, Holger
ns1:dc.dc:subject: ddc:004
ns1:dc.dc:description: This paper explores the transformation of the Enterprise Architecture (EA) function within a global life sciences company’s IT department. It highlights the shift from centralized governance to a decentralized, agile model that empowers platform teams and accelerates decision-making. Key mechanisms such as guardrails, Architecture Decision Records (ADRs), and Architecture Fitness Functions support transparency and alignment. The study offers a blueprint for modern EA governance that balances autonomy with strategic coherence.

ns1:dc.dc:title: Strategische Steuerung von wertstiftenden KI-Projekten
ns1:dc.dc:creator: Klos, Tim
Prinz, Niculin
ns1:dc.dc:subject: ddc:004
ns1:dc.dc:description: Künstliche Intelligenz (KI) gilt als Treiber der digitalen Transformation. Viele KI-Projekte scheitern jedoch an mangelnder Zusammenarbeit zwischen Fachbereichen und IT, fehlendem Verständnis für KI-Funktionalitäten und der fehlenden Fähigkeit, Aufwand und Mehr-wert realistisch einzuschätzen. Dieser Beitrag stellt einen Prozess vor, der Unternehmen bei der strategischen Identifikation, Bewertung und Priorisierung wertschöpfender KI-Projekte unterstützt. Er erweitert den Forschungsdiskurs zu Enterprise Architecture Management um einen Bezugsrahmen für die Zusammenarbeit von Fachbereichen und IT und bietet der Praxis in diesem Kontext einen transparenten Überblick über das IT-Projektportfolio sowie eine Orientierungshilfe für die strukturierte Planung und Steuerung von KI-Initiativen.

ns1:dc.dc:title: Effects of Circular Economy in the Manufacturing Industry and Underlying Information Systems
ns1:dc.dc:creator: Mayer, Arne
Braunreuther, Stefan
Öfele, Marcel
ns1:dc.dc:subject: ddc:62
ns1:dc.dc:description: As global markets increasingly demand sustainability and circular economy concepts are on the rise, products have to change as well. In this state of research presentation, we discuss effects of circular economy concepts (CE) on production and business potential for producers. The main focus is on the effects on production and manufacturing techniques, and the support of information and communication technology (ICT) systems. The examination of this twin transformation approach addressing sustainability and change of ICT systems is in the early strategic phase based on the intention to use methods from Enterprise Architecture Management.

ns1:dc.dc:title: Vorwort zum Workshop-Band Enterprise Architecture (Management) – Forschung, Lehre, Praxis
ns1:dc.dc:creator: Zimmermann, Stephan
Rentrop, Christopher
Drews, Paul
Masjosthusmann, Florian
ns1:dc.dc:subject: ddc:004
ns1:dc.dc:description: Der im Rahmen der WI 2025 angebotene Workshop „Enterprise Architecture (Management) – Forschung, Lehre, Praxis“ bot eine Plattform für den Austausch zwischen Wissenschaft und Praxis zu den Themen Enterprise Architecture und Enterprise Architecture Management (EAM). Drei Beiträge wurden durch ein Peer-Review-Verfahren der Workshop- Chairs geprüft und akzeptiert. Sie behandeln Themen wie Kreislaufwirtschaft und Informationssysteme, strategische Steuerung von KI-Projekten sowie Taxonomien für Datenmanagement im EAM-Kontext. Ergänzend gaben zwei Idea Abstracts Impulse zur Rolle von EAM in globalen IT-Transformationen und zu EAM-Service-Modellen im Rahmen des Wokrshops. Ziel der Workshopreihe ist es, aktuelle Forschungsergebnisse und praxisnahe Ansätze zu diskutieren und gemeinsam die zukünftige Forschungsagenda zu gestalten.

ns1:dc.dc:title: Time Series Analysis for Early Detection of Operational Irregularities in a Gas-Fired Material Hardening Facility Using Sensor Data
ns1:dc.dc:creator: Biswas, Anish
ns1:dc.dc:subject: ddc:004
ns1:dc.dc:description: Industrial gas-fired material hardening facilities operate under controlled temperature, pressure, and gas flow conditions to ensure consistent and reliable processing outcomes. Recording continuous streams of sensor data from multiple sources allows for the detection of anomalies in the respective time series data. These anomalies can indicate malfunctions causing unexpected downtime, lower product quality, and higher energy use. This bachelor thesis pursues two primary objectives: first, to evaluate systematic anomaly detection methods to identify operational irregularities early enough to support predictive maintenance; and second, to develop a framework that enables structured access to historical sensor data for visualization, analysis, and monitoring purposes. The study begins with the facility’s historical sensor-reading archive and explores how analytical techniques can augment traditional monitoring and enhance the spectrum of failure modes that can be identified. After modelling typical operating trends using a representative selection of anomaly detection approaches, these methods will be tested to identify variations that might indicate failure modes. Each method is assessed based on its accuracy, detection speed, and ability to generate meaningful alerts. These alerts might be helpful for the prevention of problems that may arise during production and maintenance procedures. Outlining whether analytical data detection can significantly reduce unplanned downtimes and quality losses in gas-fired hardening plants is the main objective. This will be done alongside a comparative evaluation of anomaly detection methods and the integration of the most effective model into the plant’s sensor-monitoring system.

ns1:dc.dc:title: Proceedings of the Doctoral Colloquium DigiTech
ns1:dc.dc:creator: Lermer, Eva
Timinger, Holger
Zink, Roland
ns1:dc.dc:subject: ddc:370
ns1:dc.dc:description: Das Promotionszentrum Digitale Technologien und Ihre Anwendung (DigiTech) startet mit dem vorliegenden ersten Band die Reihe „Proceedings of the Doctoral Colloquium DigiTech”. Die Reihe präsentiert zentrale Arbeiten des Promotionszentrums. Band eins geht in einem einführenden Beitrag von Roland Zink, Eva Lermer und Holger Timinger auf die Entstehung, die Struktur und das Forschungsprofil des Zentrums ein sowie auf das Promotionsrecht an Bayerischen Hochschulen für Angewandte Wissenschaften. Der Beitrag wurde zudem ins Englische übersetzt. Fachlich startet die Reihe mit zwei Forschungsbeiträgen der ersten Promovierendenkohorte.

The Doctoral Center Digital Technologies and their Application (DigiTech) starts with the first volume of the series “Proceedings of the Doctoral Colloquium DigiTech.” The series brings together key works from the Doctoral Center DigiTech. Volume 1 starts with a contribution by Roland Zink, Eva Lermer and Holger Timinger outlining the centre's origins, structure, and research profile, as well as doctoral programs at Bavarian Universities of Applied Sciences. The contribution is an English translation of the German version in this volume. The volume then continues with two research contributions by the first cohort of doctoral students.

ns1:dc.dc:title: Psychologische Evaluation KI-gestützter Entscheidungsassistenz: Einflussfaktoren, Belastungswirkung und Implikationen für die Praxis
ns1:dc.dc:creator: Fischer, Maximilian
ns1:dc.dc:subject: ddc:15
ns1:dc.dc:description: Die Arbeit auf Intensivstationen ist durch eine hohe psychische Beanspruchung des medizinischen Personals gekennzeichnet, wobei die Kapazitätssteuerung einen wesentlichen Stressfaktor darstellt. KI-gestützte Entscheidungsassistenzsysteme bergen das Potenzial, Ärzt:innen bei diesen Aufgaben zu entlasten. Diese Bachelorarbeit untersucht am Beispiel des KISIK-Systems, wie Ärzt:innen ein solches System im klinischen Alltag erleben und welche Faktoren eine beanspruchungsreduzierende Implementierung begünstigen oder behindern. Mittels eines sequenziellen Mixed-Methods-Designs wurde zunächst eine quantitative Umfrage unter nutzenden Ärzt:innen durchgeführt und im Rahmen einer multiplen Fallanalyse ausgewertet und anschließend durch ein qualitatives Interview mit einem Stationsarzt vertieft. Die Ergebnisse zeigen eine ambivalente Wahrnehmung des Systems. Es konnten drei unterschiedliche Nutzenden Personas identifiziert werden: die idealen Profiteur:innen, die Überforderten und die unbeeindruckten Pragmatiker:innen, was zeigt, dass die Wirkung des Systems stark von individuellen und kontextuellen Faktoren abhängt. Es wird geschlussfolgert, dass der Erfolg eines KI EAS weniger von der technischen Funktionalität als vom soziotechnischen Zusammenspiel aus individuellen Faktoren, Systemeigenschaften und organisationalen Rahmenbedingungen abhängt.

Work on intensive care units is characterized by high levels of mental workload for medical staff, with capacity management representing a significant stress factor. AI-based decision support systems have the potential to relieve physicians of these tasks. Using the example of the KISIK system, this bachelor thesis examines how physicians experience such a system in everyday clinical practice and which factors promote or hinder an implementation that reduces workload. Using a sequential mixed-methods design, a quantitative survey among using physicians was first conducted and evaluated as part of a multiple case analysis, followed by an in-depth qualitative interview with a ward physician. The results show an ambivalent perception of the system. Three different user personas were identified: the ideal beneficiaries, the overwhelmed, and the unimpressed pragmatists, which demonstrates that the system's impact depends heavily on individual and contextual factors. It is concluded that the success of an AI decision support system depends less on technical functionality than on the sociotechnical interplay of individual factors, system characteristics, and organizational framework conditions.

ns1:dc.dc:title: Phy-gitales Design Myzel-basierte Verbundswerkstoffe und der digitale Produktpass
ns1:dc.dc:creator: Doll, Florian
ns1:dc.dc:subject: ddc:62
ns1:dc.dc:description: Die Transformation zur Circular Economy erfordert einen fundamentalen Wandel in der Wahrnehmung von Stoffkreisläufen. Während Biomaterialien wie Myzelkomposite ein enormes ökologisches Potenzial bieten, bleibt ihre materielle Herkunft für Konsument:innen oft unsichtbar. Diese Bachelor-Thesis entwickelt und evaluiert ein phygitales System, das lokal gewachsene Myzel-Artefakte mit einer narrativen digitalen Schnittstelle – der „Myzel-Map“ – verknüpft. Methodisch folgt die Arbeit dem Researchthrough- Design-Ansatz. Im Rahmen eines experimentellen DIY-Labors wurden lokale Reststoffe (Hanf, Stroh, Sägespäne) mit dem Pilzstamm Pleurotus ostreatus kultiviert. Die Arbeit interpretiert den kommenden Digitalen Produktpass (DPP) neu: Nicht als rein technokratische Datenbank, sondern als narratives Werkzeug zur Förderung kultureller Wertschätzung. Ergebnisse zeigen, dass erst die Verschränkung sensorischer Materialerfahrung und digitaler Kontextualisierung einen tiefgreifenden Diskurs über Zirkularität katalysiert. Das Artefakt macht dabei die notwendige Care-Arbeit hinter biologischen Wachstumsprozessen sichtbar. Die Thesis plädiert abschließend für ein „Scaling Out“ – ein Modell dezentraler, vernetzter Fertigung als resiliente Alternative zu globalisierten Industriestrukturen.

The transition to a circular economy requires a fundamental shift in how we perceive material cycles. While biomaterials such as mycelium-based composites offer significant ecological potential, their origin and manufacturing processes often remain invisible to consumers. This thesis develops and evaluates a phygital system linking locally grown mycelium artifacts with a narrative digital interface: the "Myzel-Map". Adopting a Research through Design methodology, various local waste streams (hemp, straw, sawdust) were cultivated with Pleurotus ostreatus in a DIY lab setting. The project reinterprets the upcoming Digital Product Passport (DPP) as a tool for appreciation and narrative transparency rather than mere technocratic data. Workshop results demonstrate that the synergy of sensory material experience and digital contextualization acts as a powerful catalyst for discourse on circularity. The artifact reveals the essential "care work" behind biological growth. Finally, the thesis advocates for "Scaling Out"—a model of decentralized, networked manufacturing as a resilient alternative to global industrial structures.

ns1:dc.dc:title: Evaluation and Integration of the Linux Integrity Subsystem on Embedded Linux Systems
ns1:dc.dc:creator: Almislmani, Yazan
ns1:dc.dc:subject: ddc:004
ns1:dc.dc:description:
Embedded Linux devices are susceptible to offline tampering and supply chain manipulation, rendering the integrity of persistent executables and shared libraries a practical security concern. This thesis integrates the Linux Integrity Subsystem into a Yocto-based embedded Linux image on a Raspberry Pi 4 and evaluates runtime integrity controls for persistent root filesystem artifacts.
A controlled configuration ladder is implemented, ranging from a baseline without integrity mechanisms to IMA measurement, IMA appraisal, and appraisal combined with EVM metadata protection. Two policy profiles are examined: a strict profile extending appraisal to file reads in addition to execution and executable memory mapping, and a minimal profile restricted to binaries and shared libraries.
Results indicate that policy scope dominates performance overhead. Under the strict profile, sequential and random read throughput decrease significantly, while boot time increases moderately. Furthermore, EVM prevents metadata forgery that can bypass hash-based appraisal.

ns1:dc.dc:title: KI in Coaching und Training
ns1:dc.dc:creator: Hatfield, Sarah
Gredel, Christoph
ns1:dc.dc:subject: ddc:65
ns1:dc.dc:description: Die Studie der Technischen Hochschule Augsburg und changeXperten untersucht den Einsatz von Künstlicher Intelligenz im Training und Coaching im deutschsprachigen Raum im Jahr 2025. Grundlage der Studie sind 21 leitfadengestützte Interviews mit Expertinnen und Experten aus Beratung, Human Resources, Coaching und Management, ausgewertet mittels qualitativer Inhaltsanalyse. Die Ergebnisse zeigen, dass KI derzeit überwiegend als unterstützendes Werkzeug genutzt wird, insbesondere für Recherche, Konzeptentwicklung, administrative Aufgaben sowie Reflexion und Ideengenerierung. Mit Blick auf die Zukunft erwarten die Befragten eine stärkere Integration von KI in Trainings- und Coachingprozesse, wobei standardisierte Formate weiter digitalisiert werden. Gleichzeitig bleibt menschliches Coaching vor allem bei komplexen und emotionalen Themen zentral. Insgesamt zeichnet sich eine funktionale Arbeitsteilung zwischen KI und Mensch ab, deren Gestaltung künftig eine der Kernaufgaben von HR-Abteilungen sein wird, wofür neue Rollenkonzepte definiert werden müssen.
